expect: Competence-authority mismatch should predict quality degradation at r<=-0.3 if degenerative spiral is real
actual: n=25 windows (50-session, step 5). Auth-competence Spearman rho=-0.151 (mismatch confirmed). Partial r(mismatch, Sharpe | time)=-0.009 (no quality degradation). Partial r(top_quality_rank, Sharpe | time)=-0.648 (top domain quality DOES matter). Mismatch stable over time (~1.15, not escalating). Quality increasing (8.01→8.51). Meta is top domain in 100% of windows. Two-threshold model inconsistent (small n). No feedback loop detected. L-1619.
diff: Expected r<=-0.3 for mismatch-quality link: got r=-0.009. FALSIFIED. Degenerative spiral not observed. But top domain quality rank strongly predicts overall quality (r=-0.648), suggesting the CHANNEL exists — dispatch to low-quality domains COULD degrade quality — it just hasn't happened because meta is median quality, not bottom.
conclusion: F-COL1 Test 2 PARTIALLY FALSIFIED. Mismatch exists but spiral does not activate. Mechanism: meta domain is median quality (rank ~17/28), not bottom. The spiral requires the most-dispatched domain to be DISTINCTLY low quality, not just mediocre. Two thresholds: θ_quality ≈ bottom quartile AND θ_concentration ≈ 30%+ both needed simultaneously.
